behavior_summary: Provides a small per-named-sorcerer flag system (for Cardas, Daemos, Kothius, Mentar, Tallon, Emrichol) and uses those flags to shape short barks and full dialogue when the player interacts with sorcerer NPCs.

Exposes setter helpers that map an input name string to a specific global byte flag and store a supplied value (used to record whether the player 'met' a sorcerer, whether a name form is known, job/profession variants, and avatar-name variants).

Implements brief-info/bark selection and a larger dialogue handler that read per-name flags plus NPC runtime state (dead/alive, status bits) and global story flags (e.g., acolyteKilled) to choose appropriate spoken lines, open/close dialog gumps, and spawn auxiliary behaviors/animations.
notable_control_flow: Name-based dispatch: each setter routine compares the incoming name_string to a fixed list of sorcerer names and on match writes the provided byte to the corresponding global flag; returns the stored byte or 0 if no match.

Dialogue branching: U8P_SORCERER::039C and U8P_SORCERER::05EC load the per-name flags (<name>Met, <name>Name, <name>Job, <name>AvaName) and branch on those values plus Npc::isDead() and the acolyteKilled global to choose different bark/dialogue text.
UI guard logic: Item::getStatus / openGump / closeGump status bits are inspected to open/close interaction windows as part of the dialogue flow.

Asynchronous actions: the dialogue handler spawns METHOD::<id> routines (e.g., 0BCF, 1090, 15E7) and queries Npc::getDir before spawning, indicating direction-based animations or queued conversation subroutines.
open_questions: Whether U8P_SORCERER::05EC directly calls U8P_SORCERER::039C as a helper: the analysis suggests reuse between the brief bark helper and the full dialogue handler but the direct call graph is inferred, not canonical.
Precise behavior and side effects of FREE::1A85 and the spawned METHOD::<id> routines (0BCF, 1090, 15E7) — their inner logic and impact on dialogue/animation are unknown from this module alone.
Exact mapping of the status bits inspected via Item::getStatus (what bit values correspond to 'gump open' vs other states) is inferred rather than explicitly documented here.

main_function.name: U8P_SORCERER::0080 (setMetFlag_byName)
main_function.purpose: Given a sorcerer name string and a byte value, match the name to one of the known sorcerers and set that sorcerer's '<name>Met' global flag to the provided byte; return the stored byte or 0 if no match.
entry_conditions: Called whenever game logic wants to record whether the player has 'met' a particular named sorcerer; input parameters are name_string (char*) and value_byte (byte).
Performs direct string comparisons against the known sorcerer names and updates the matching global.

function_map.name: U8P_SORCERER::0080 (setMetFlag_byName)
function_map.role: Name-to-global-flag setter for '<name>Met' flags; primary setter exposed by the module.

function_map.name: U8P_SORCERER::039C (bark_and_briefInfo)
function_map.role: Construct and emit a short label/bark for a named sorcerer by reading stored per-name flags and NPC state; performs Item::bark with the selected short string.

function_map.name: U8P_SORCERER::05EC (dialogue_interaction_handler)
function_map.role: Main interaction/dialogue handler for Sorcerer NPCs: reads per-name flags and story flags, checks NPC runtime/status, chooses full dialogue lines, manages gumps, and spawns additional behaviors/animations.

internal_calls.purpose: Inferred reuse: short-info barks are likely produced via the brief-info helper as part of the full dialogue flow (selection and emission of short labels).
Inferred: dialogue flow may set 'met' flags when appropriate during interaction; presence is plausible but not explicitly shown in snippet.

external_calls.purpose: Emit the constructed short label/bark string to the triggering player/process.
Check whether the NPC is dead to select living vs. 'late' dialogue variants.
Read NPC/item status bits to decide whether to open/close gumps or alter dialogue flow.
Open a dialog/gump for the NPC interaction when conditions demand richer UI.
Close the NPC's gump when dialogue or state requires it.
Retrieve NPC facing direction prior to spawning direction-based animations or actions.
Asynchronously launch animations, queued subroutines, or conversation sub-behaviors tied to the dialogue sequence.
Utility/freeing or small-state helper used conditionally in dialogue logic; exact role unclear.
Set routine-local context/info fields on the NPC/process at helper start (used to annotate processing context).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides a small per-named-sorcerer flag system (for Cardas, Daemos, Kothius, Mentar, Tallon, Emrichol) and uses those flags to shape short barks and full dialogue when the player interacts with sorcerer NPCs.
